target.name: StoreDot
target.founder: Doron Myersdorf, Simon Litsyn, Gil Rosenman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $206M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
StoreDot is a Series E climate and energy technology company founded by Doron Myersdorf, Simon Litsyn, and Gil Rosenman that has raised $206.5M to date. The Climate & Energy sector comprises 9 peer companies, including 1 public entity (Linamar, LNZA) and 8 private firms such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at similar valuation tiers to support a reliable median multiple approach.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation method. Using $206.5M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement yields an implied valuation of $289M. This represents the median point in the analysis.

The quantitative range supported by available methods spans $246M (low) to $332M (high), with a median of $289M. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer benchmarking, which limits confidence in relative positioning within the Climate & Energy sector. The reliance on stage-progression as the sole method introduces sensitivity to the assumed 1.4x multiplier. Validation of this range would benefit from access to recent transaction data or financial metrics (revenue, burn rate, growth trajectory) for same-stage energy technology companies.
